name: vizsla-lsp
description: Language support for Verilog and SystemVerilog powered by the Vizsla language server.

vizsla.trace.server.description: Traces the communication between VS Code and the language server.

vizsla.server.command.description: Override command used to launch the Vizsla language server. Leave empty to use the bundled binary.

vizsla.server.args.description: Arguments passed before any additional arguments when a custom command is provided.

vizsla.server.cwd.description: Working directory for the Vizsla language server process. Defaults to the first workspace folder.

vizsla.server.additionalArgs.description: Additional arguments appended when launching the Vizsla language server.

vizsla.qihe.command.description: Command used to invoke Qihe. It must be available in the environment PATH seen by VS Code.

vizsla.qihe.compileArgs.description: Arguments inserted after `qihe compile`. Use this for compile mode selection or forwarded `slang` options such as `--mode sv -- -I include`.

vizsla.qihe.runArgs.description: Arguments inserted after `qihe run` when the Qihe analysis button is used.

vizsla.files.excludeDirs.markdownDescription: Workspace-relative directories ignored by Vizsla. Globs are not supported; add matching patterns to VS Code `files.watcherExclude` if needed.

enumDescriptions: Use VS Code watched-file notifications when supported.
vizsla.files.watcher.description: Controls how Vizsla watches project files.

vizsla.workspace.auto.reload.description: Automatically refresh project information when project manifests change.

vizsla.scope.visibility.description: Controls whether symbols inside scopes, except ports, are visible outside those scopes.

enumDescriptions: Use an external verible-verilog-format executable. Supports Format Document and Format Selection.

vizsla.formatter.path.description: Path to verible-verilog-format when vizsla.formatter.provider is verible. Leave empty to find it on PATH.

vizsla.formatter.args.description: Arguments passed to verible-verilog-format when vizsla.formatter.provider is verible.

vizsla.formatting.on.enter.description: Enable formatting behavior when pressing Enter.

vizsla.formatting.indent.width.description: Fallback indentation width used when editor formatting options are unavailable.

vizsla.inlayHints.port.connection.enable.description: Show inlay hints for port connections.

vizsla.inlayHints.parameter.assignment.e.description: Show inlay hints for parameter assignments.

vizsla.inlayHints.end.structure.enable.description: Show inlay hints for ending structure names.

vizsla.lens.instantiations.enable.description: Show code lenses for module instantiations.

vizsla.semantic.tokens.port.clk.rst.enab.description: Highlight clock and reset ports with dedicated semantic token modifiers.

vizsla.semantic.tokens.port.input.output.description: Highlight input and output ports with dedicated semantic token modifiers.

vizsla.diagnostics.update.markdownDescription: Controls when diagnostics are refreshed. `onSave` avoids running expensive diagnostics on every edit; `onType` refreshes diagnostics after document changes.

vizsla.diagnostics.slang.warnings.markdownDescription: slang warning options, equivalent to command-line warning names such as `default`, `everything`, `none`, `error`, `no-<name>`, or `error=<name>`.

vizsla.diagnostics.slang.rules.markdownDescription: Rules that filter or override diagnostic severities. Supported selectors: `code:<subsystem>:<code>`, `option:<name>`, `group:<name>`, `source:parse`, `source:semantic`.

severity.description: Severity to apply when this rule matches. Use ignore to hide the diagnostic.

force.description: Reserved for future support for overriding in-source diagnostic pragmas.

vizsla.signature.help.params.only.description: Show signature help for parameters only.

scripts.compile: npm run clean && npm run typecheck && npm run bundle
scripts.package:alpine-arm64: npm run compile && tsx scripts/package.ts alpine-arm64
scripts.package:alpine-x64: npm run compile && tsx scripts/package.ts alpine-x64
scripts.package:darwin-arm64: npm run compile && tsx scripts/package.ts darwin-arm64
scripts.package:darwin-x64: npm run compile && tsx scripts/package.ts darwin-x64
scripts.package:linux-x64: npm run compile && tsx scripts/package.ts linux-x64
scripts.package:linux-arm64: npm run compile && tsx scripts/package.ts linux-arm64
scripts.package:win32-x64: npm run compile && tsx scripts/package.ts win32-x64
scripts.package:win32-arm64: npm run compile && tsx scripts/package.ts win32-arm64
